items.hr: Otvorena pitanja: odgovor je u tekstu, ne u tvojoj glavi.
items.en: Open questions: the answer is in the text, not in your head.
items.note: For 'Zašto…?' or 'Gdje…?', quote the text. The examiner rewards what the passage says, not your general knowledge.

items.en: Gap-fill: 'in the middle of ___' takes the genitive → sredinom srpnja.
items.note: sredinom / početkom / krajem + genitive. The frame word before the gap decides the case — read it, don't guess by meaning.

items.hr: Popunjavanje: 'na ___' za mjesto traži lokativ → na otvorenom.
items.en: Gap-fill: 'na ___' for place takes the locative → na otvorenom.
items.note: na + locative = static place (na otvorenom = in the open air). na + accusative would be motion — check whether it's position or direction.

items.hr: Popunjavanje: broj 5+ traži genitiv množine → pet godina, tisuću izvođača.
items.en: Gap-fill: numbers 5+ take the genitive plural → pet godina, a thousand performers.
items.note: After 5 and up (and pola, mnogo, tisuću) → genitive plural: godina, izvođača. A guaranteed gap-fill trap.

items.hr: Popunjavanje: prijedlog 's/sa' traži instrumental → s lijepim pogledom.
items.en: Gap-fill: the preposition 's/sa' takes the instrumental → with a nice view.
items.note: s + instrumental; the adjective agrees: s lijepim pogledom. Match the case of the frame, then decline the adjective to it.

items.hr: Ne zapinji na jednoj nepoznatoj riječi — nastavi, smisao se često vidi iz konteksta.
items.en: Don't get stuck on one unknown word — read on; the sense is often clear from context.
items.note: Speed matters in the biggest section. One unknown word rarely blocks the answer; the surrounding sentence usually gives it away.

items.hr: Provjeri padež u odgovoru: prepiši oblik, ne osnovni oblik riječi.
items.en: Check the case in your answer: copy the form, not the dictionary form.
items.note: In the gap-fill you write the inflected word (srpnja, godina), never the base (srpanj, godina-nominative). The form is the mark.

activities.title: Čitaj tekst i popuni prazninu
activities.intro: Both reading genres, timed: first answer open questions on a short biography, then work a word-bank gap-fill where every gap is a case trap. Read the frame word before each gap — it, not the meaning, tells you the ending. Difficulty rises 1 to 9.

questions.prompt: ČITANJE — Pročitajte: 'Ivana Brlić-Mažuranić rođena je 1874. u Ogulinu. Bila je hrvatska književnica, poznata po pričama za djecu. Napisala je Priče iz davnine, a mnogi je zovu hrvatskim Andersenom.' — Gdje je rođena Ivana Brlić-Mažuranić?
questions.explanation: 'rođena je 1874. u Ogulinu' — she was born in Ogulin.

questions.prompt: ČITANJE — (isti tekst) Po čemu je bila poznata?
questions.explanation: 'poznata po pričama za djecu' — famous for children's stories.

questions.prompt: ČITANJE — (isti tekst) Koje je godine rođena Ivana Brlić-Mažuranić? (upišite godinu)
questions.explanation: 'rođena je 1874.' — the year is 1874.

questions.prompt: ČITANJE — (isti tekst) Kako je mnogi zovu?
questions.explanation: 'mnogi je zovu hrvatskim Andersenom' — many call her the Croatian Andersen (instrumental after zvati).

questions.prompt: POPUNJAVANJE — 'Biljka počinje cvjetati sredinom ___.' (srpanj)
questions.explanation: sredinom + genitive → srpanj → srpnja (with the fleeting a dropping).

questions.prompt: POPUNJAVANJE — 'Ne preporučuje se boravak na ___ rano ujutro.' (otvoreno)
questions.explanation: na + locative for place: na otvorenom = in the open air.

questions.prompt: POPUNJAVANJE — 'Stanujem ovdje već pet ___.' (godina)
questions.explanation: Numbers 5 and up take the genitive plural: pet godina.

questions.prompt: POPUNJAVANJE — 'Tražim stan s ___ pogledom na more.' (lijep)
questions.explanation: s + instrumental; the adjective agrees in the instrumental: s lijepim pogledom.

questions.prompt: POPUNJAVANJE — 'Koncert počinje početkom ___.' (rujan → genitiv)
questions.explanation: početkom + genitive → rujan → rujna (fleeting a drops). The frame word početkom forces the genitive.

days.title: Weak-section day: grammar re-drill
days.objective: Re-drill every grammar structure you've missed since day 216.
days.paretoFocus: Your error log knows exactly which structures leak points. Fix the leaks, not the whole ocean.
drills: Re-do every grammar item marked wrong in your error log.
Words tab: verb-families pack — target the 3 wobbliest families.
Write 6 sentences using your personal top-3 error structures correctly.
Do one fresh grammar drill in the mock exam in the Quiz tab.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
State your top-3 grammar rules in your own words.
Check off fixed errors in the log.

activities.title: Gramatika — ponovi strukture koje cure bodove
activities.intro: Grammar is a scored section and it rewards precision, not breadth. These eight structures are the ones B1 candidates most often miss: numbers + genitive plural, the instrumental of habit, futur II after kad, the standard bismo/biste, među vs između, jer vs zbog, stressed vs clitic pronouns after prepositions, and the comparative adverb. Re-drill exactly these — your error log tells you which leak most.

items.hr: Hrvatska ima oko četiri milijuna stanovnika.
items.en: Croatia has about four million inhabitants.
items.note: Numbers above 4 and milijun take the genitive plural: stanovnika. This is a near-guaranteed grammar item.

items.en: On Thursdays I always play tennis.
items.note: The instrumental of time = a regular pattern: četvrtkom = on Thursdays, subotom = on Saturdays. Not 'u četvrtak' (one specific day).

items.hr: Kad budem imao vremena, nazvat ću te.
items.en: When I have time, I'll call you.
items.note: Future time clause after kad → futur II (budem + participle), NOT futur I. The main clause stays futur I: nazvat ću.

items.hr: I mi bismo rado išli na utakmicu!
items.en: We'd gladly go to the match too!
items.note: 1st person plural conditional is bismo, not colloquial bi. The exam tests exactly this: bismo (we), biste (you pl.).

items.hr: Naočale su među knjigama na polici.
items.en: The glasses are among the books on the shelf.
items.note: među + instrumental = among (more than two); između + genitive = between (two). Choose by how many things there are.

items.hr: Zakasnila je jer se tramvaj pokvario.
items.en: She was late because the tram broke down.
items.note: jer introduces a clause with a verb; zbog takes a noun in the genitive (zbog kvara). Match the connector to what follows.

items.hr: Čuli smo samo lijepo o njemu.
items.en: We heard only good things about him.
items.note: After a preposition use the STRESSED pronoun: o njemu, s njim, za nju — never the clitic (mu, mi, ga).

items.hr: Ljepše je živjeti na moru nego u gradu.
items.en: It's nicer to live by the sea than in the city.
items.note: Comparative ADVERB ljepše (nicer, how it is), not the adjective ljepši. nego signals the comparison.

activities.title: Ponovni pogon — gramatika 1 do 9
activities.intro: Nine grammar items straight from the exam's favourite traps: genitive plural after numbers, the instrumental of habit, futur II, the standard bismo, među/između, and the stressed pronoun after a preposition. Diacritics count. Fix the leaks your log flagged.

questions.prompt: Hrvatska ima oko četiri milijuna ___.
questions.explanation: milijun and numbers above 4 take the genitive plural: stanovnika.

questions.prompt: ___ uvijek igram tenis. (on Thursdays — a regular pattern)
questions.explanation: The instrumental of time expresses habit: četvrtkom = on Thursdays.

questions.prompt: Nema više ___ u hladnjaku. (mlijeko → genitiv)
questions.explanation: nema + genitive: mlijeko → mlijeka.

questions.prompt: Naočale su ___ knjigama na polici. (among several books)
questions.explanation: među + instrumental = among (more than two things); između + genitive = between two.

questions.prompt: Kad ___ vremena, nazvat ću te. (imati → futur II, ja, m. r.)
questions.explanation: Future time clause after kad → futur II: budem + participle → budem imao.

questions.prompt: I mi ___ rado išli na utakmicu! (standard conditional)
questions.explanation: 1st person plural conditional is bismo, not colloquial bi; biste is for Vi.

questions.prompt: Zakasnila je ___ se tramvaj pokvario. (because + clause)
questions.explanation: jer introduces a causal clause with a verb; zbog takes a noun in the genitive.

questions.prompt: Čuli smo samo lijepo o ___. (about him — after a preposition)
questions.explanation: After a preposition use the stressed form: o njemu. The clitic mu cannot follow a preposition.

questions.prompt: Say it: It's nicer to live by the sea than in the city.
questions.answer: Ljepše je živjeti na moru nego u gradu
questions.explanation: Comparative adverb Ljepše + je + infinitive; na moru / u gradu = locative; nego marks the comparison.

days.title: Writing polish: both genres back-to-back
days.objective: Both writing tasks in one 40-minute sitting — like the real exam.
days.paretoFocus: On exam day you write opis + životopis in one session. Train the stamina and the switch between genres.
drills: 40-minute timer: picture description THEN životopis, from the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
Self-check both against rubrics; count sentences (7-8 each).
Fix diacritics with a coloured pen — make errors visible.
One native check on the weaker text.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Which genre cost more time? Plan the split.
Rewrite your single weakest sentence.

activities.title: Oba pisanja zaredom — izdržljivost i prebacivanje žanra
activities.intro: On exam day you write both texts — opis slike and životopis — in one 40-minute session. Today trains the stamina and the switch between genres: present tense and scene words for the picture, perfekt and life-facts for the CV. Keep two mental checklists and split the time cleanly: 20 minutes each, 2 minutes to reread for gender and diacritics. The items below are the switch-points and the shared rubric.

items.hr: Opis slike: sadašnje vrijeme. 'Na slici vidim… Djeca se igraju…'
items.en: Picture description: present tense. 'In the picture I see… The children are playing…'
items.note: Genre 1 lives in the present: vidim, sjede, razgovaraju. Describe what is happening now in the frame.

items.hr: Životopis: perfekt i činjenice. 'Rođen sam… Završio sam… Radio sam…'
items.en: CV: perfekt and facts. 'I was born… I finished… I worked…'
items.note: Genre 2 lives in the past: rođen sam, završio sam, radio sam — all masculine. Switching tense is the whole trick today.

items.hr: Prebacivanje: kad završiš sliku, svjesno prijeđi na 'ja' i prošlost.
items.en: Switching: when you finish the picture, consciously shift to 'I' and the past.
items.note: The commonest stamina error is dragging present tense into the CV. Pause two seconds and reset the tense before task 2.

items.hr: Vrijeme: 20 min slika + 20 min životopis + 2 min provjera svakog.
items.en: Time: 20 min picture + 20 min CV + 2 min to check each.
items.note: Don't let task 1 eat task 2's minutes. If the picture runs long, cut it at eight sentences and move on — both must be finished.

items.hr: Veznici drže tekst: jer, a, dok (slika); nakon, a, pa, koji (životopis).
items.en: Connectors hold the text together: jer, a, dok (picture); nakon, a, pa, koji (CV).
items.note: A few connectors turn a list into a text in both genres. One relative koji/koja in the CV lifts the register.

items.hr: Provjeri: 7-8 rečenica u svakom tekstu — prebroji ih.
items.en: Check: 7-8 sentences in each text — count them.
items.note: Shared rubric point. Length is scored in both tasks; count before you stop, don't estimate.

items.hr: Provjeri: rod u perfektu (životopis) i sadašnje vrijeme (slika).
items.en: Check: gender in the perfekt (CV) and present tense (picture).
items.note: The genre-specific traps: masculine -o participles in the CV, consistent present in the picture. Reread each for its own trap.

items.hr: Provjeri zadnje: svaki č, ć, š, ž, đ — jedno čitanje samo za dijakritiku.
items.en: Check last: every č, ć, š, ž, đ — one reading just for diacritics.
items.note: The cheapest marks on the paper. One final pass in both texts, looking only at the hooks, catches most spelling losses.

activities.title: Prebaci žanr — sastavi oba teksta
activities.intro: Switch cleanly between the two writing genres: pick the right tense for each, drop in the connector, and reorder lines from both a picture description and a CV. Get the present/perfekt switch and the masculine agreement automatic, and 40 minutes will be plenty. Difficulty rises 1 to 9.

questions.prompt: Which tense belongs in a picture description?
questions.explanation: An opis slike describes what is happening now → present tense: vidim, sjede, razgovaraju.

questions.prompt: Which opening belongs to a CV, not a picture description?
questions.explanation: Rođen sam… is a CV fact in the perfekt. The others are present-tense picture sentences.

questions.prompt: SLIKA — Majka drži čašu soka, ___ otac čita novine. (while — two simultaneous actions)
questions.explanation: dok = while, joins two simultaneous present-tense actions in the picture.

questions.prompt: ŽIVOTOPIS — Nakon studija ___ sam pet godina u Zagrebu. (raditi — perfekt, ja, m. r.)
questions.explanation: CV switches to the perfekt: raditi → masculine participle radio.

questions.prompt: You just finished the picture and start the CV. Which sentence shows the correct tense switch?
options: Zovem se Ivan i rodim se 1988.
Zovem se Ivan i rođen sam 1988.
Zovem se Ivan i rađam se 1988.
Zovem se Ivan i rodio se 1988.
questions.answer: Zovem se Ivan i rođen sam 1988.
questions.explanation: The CV needs the perfekt fact rođen sam (1st person, masculine). The present-tense forms and 3rd-person rodio se are wrong.

questions.prompt: SLIKA — Say it: In the picture I see a young family in a park.
questions.answer: Na slici vidim mladu obitelj u parku
questions.explanation: Na slici vidim + accusative (mladu obitelj); u parku = locative of place. Present tense for the picture.

questions.prompt: ŽIVOTOPIS — Tražim posao u ___ mogu koristiti svoje znanje. (koji → lokativ, m. r.)
questions.explanation: u + locative of koji → u kojem — a relative clause that lifts the CV's register.

questions.prompt: ŽIVOTOPIS — Say it: After my studies I moved to Zagreb because of family.
questions.answer: Nakon studija preselio sam se u Zagreb zbog obitelji
questions.explanation: nakon + genitive (studija); preselio sam se (reflexive perfekt, masculine); u Zagreb = direction (accusative); zbog + genitive.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is fully correct? (male candidate, CV)
options: Studirao sam informatiku i diplomirala prije osam godina.
Studirao sam informatiku i diplomirao prije osam godina.
Studirala sam informatiku i diplomirao prije osam godina.
Studirao sam informatiku i diplomirao prije osam godine.
questions.answer: Studirao sam informatiku i diplomirao prije osam godina.
questions.explanation: Both participles masculine (studirao, diplomirao) for a male candidate; prije osam godina = genitive plural after the number.

days.title: Speaking simulation #2
days.objective: Second full oral simulation — smoother, calmer, longer answers.
days.paretoFocus: Examiners reward answers of 3+ sentences. Train expanding: statement + reason + example, every time.
drills: Full speaking simulation with your tutor or Croatian spouse using the mock exam speaking prompts in the Quiz tab.
Rule: every answer gets 3+ sentences (statement, reason, example).
Record and compare with day 224 — hear the difference.
Drill your 3 hesitation points from last time.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Expand 'Volim li Hrvatsku?' into 4 sentences, aloud.
Note 2 remaining rough spots.

activities.title: Proširi odgovor: tvrdnja + razlog + primjer
activities.intro: Examiners reward answers of three or more sentences, so never stop at a bare 'da' or 'ne'. Train one reflex for every speaking prompt: state your point (tvrdnja), give a reason (razlog), then add an example (primjer). These twelve ready-made frames turn a one-word answer into a confident mini-paragraph. Say each aloud with your own content and the expanding becomes automatic on exam day.

items.en: statement → reason → example
items.note: The three-step answer. Every prompt gets: what I think, why I think it, and one concrete example. Three sentences, minimum.

items.hr: Slažem se s tim, i to zato što…
items.en: I agree with that, and that's because…
items.note: The bridge from tvrdnja to razlog: i to zato što + full clause. zato što = because (with a verb).

items.en: For example…
items.note: The signal for your primjer. Primjerice is the slightly more formal variant — perfect for the exam register.

items.en: From my own experience…
items.note: A strong way to open an example: Iz vlastitog iskustva, padeži su najteži dio. vlastiti = one's own.

items.en: Let's say… / For instance…
items.note: A lighter, conversational way to introduce an example: Recimo, jučer sam… Keeps you talking while you think.

items.en: Not only… but also…
items.note: Adds weight to a point: Učim ne samo gramatiku nego i izgovor. Both parts take the same case/structure.

items.hr: Ono što mi je najvažnije jest…
items.en: What matters most to me is…
items.note: A confident opener that raises the register. Follow with a noun or a da-clause: …jest da razumijem obitelj.

items.en: To clarify that…
items.note: Buys you a sentence and shows control: Da to pojasnim, mislim na svakodnevne razgovore. pojasniti = to clarify.

items.en: When I think about it a bit more…
items.note: A natural way to extend an answer and add a second reason instead of falling silent.

items.en: That's exactly why…
items.note: Links your example back to your point: …i baš zato volim hrvatski. Closes the loop neatly.

items.en: In short, my position is clear.
items.note: A tidy closing frame after you've given reason and example. Ukratko = in short; stav = stance, position.

items.hr: Volim planinariti jer me opušta; na primjer, subotom idem na Sljeme.
items.en: I like hiking because it relaxes me; for example, on Saturdays I go to Sljeme.
items.note: The full pattern in one line: tvrdnja (Volim planinariti) + razlog (jer me opušta) + primjer (subotom idem na Sljeme).

activities.title: Expanding every answer to 3+ sentences
activities.intro: Drill the machinery of the long answer: the connector that opens a reason, the phrase that flags an example, and the frames that let you keep talking. Build the reflex tvrdnja → razlog → primjer before the simulation.

questions.prompt: What does 'Na primjer' signal in an answer?
questions.explanation: Na primjer / Primjerice = for example — it introduces the primjer, the third step of the expanded answer.

questions.prompt: Which three-step order do examiners reward?
questions.explanation: tvrdnja → razlog → primjer: say your point, justify it, then give a concrete example.

questions.prompt: Complete the reason link: Slažem se, i to zato ______ je hrvatski koristan. ('because')
questions.explanation: zato ŠTO + verb = because. It introduces the razlog: i to zato što je hrvatski koristan.

questions.prompt: Which frame best opens a personal example?
questions.explanation: Iz vlastitog iskustva = from my own experience — a strong lead-in to a concrete example.

questions.prompt: Complete: Ne ______ gramatiku nego i izgovor. ('not only')
questions.explanation: ne samo… nego i… = not only… but also… : Ne učim samo gramatiku nego i izgovor.

questions.prompt: Complete the tidy closer: '______, moj je stav jasan.'
questions.explanation: Ukratko = in short — a closing frame after you've given a reason and an example.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'What matters most to me is the family.'
questions.answer: Ono što mi je najvažnije jest obitelj
questions.explanation: Relative frame Ono što mi je najvažnije + jest + the noun obitelj. The clitics mi je sit in second position of the relative clause.

questions.prompt: Build a full three-step answer: 'I like hiking because it relaxes me; for example, I go to Sljeme.'
questions.answer: Volim planinariti jer me opušta na primjer, idem na Sljeme
questions.explanation: tvrdnja (Volim planinariti) + razlog (jer me opušta) + primjer (na primjer, idem na Sljeme). The whole pattern in one breath.

questions.prompt: You are asked 'Volite li hrvatski?'. Which reply best fits the exam?
options: Volim ga jer zvuči lijepo; na primjer, uživam u pjesmama Olivera Dragojevića.
questions.answer: Volim ga jer zvuči lijepo; na primjer, uživam u pjesmama Olivera Dragojevića.
questions.explanation: Only the third answer gives statement + reason + example — three sentences of content, exactly what examiners reward.

activities.title: Simulacija govorenja #2
activities.intro: Your second full oral simulation: your Croatian wife plays the examiner and works through the mock exam speaking prompts. Read your lines ('Me') aloud and hold the rule — every answer gets at least three sentences: statement, reason, example. Notice how calm and long the answers feel compared with day 224.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobar dan. Za početak, predstavite se i recite nešto o sebi.
lines.en: Good afternoon. To begin, introduce yourself and tell me something about yourself.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dobar dan. Zovem se Ricardo i živim u Hrvatskoj već nekoliko godina. Učim hrvatski jer mi je žena Hrvatica, a i djecu želimo odgajati dvojezično. Na primjer, kod kuće svaki dan pola sata razgovaramo samo na hrvatskom.
lines.en: Good afternoon. My name is Ricardo and I've lived in Croatia for a few years now. I'm learning Croatian because my wife is Croatian, and we also want to raise our children bilingually. For example, at home we talk only in Croatian for half an hour every day.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vrlo lijepo. Recite mi, kako obično provodite vikend?
lines.en: Very nice. Tell me, how do you usually spend the weekend?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vikend najviše volim provoditi u prirodi jer me to opušta nakon napornog tjedna. Iz vlastitog iskustva, šetnja uz more djeluje bolje od bilo kojeg lijeka. Recimo, prošle subote smo žena i ja pješačili tri sata i vratili se sretni i umorni.
lines.en: I most love spending the weekend in nature, because it relaxes me after a hard week. From my own experience, a walk by the sea works better than any medicine. Let's say, last Saturday my wife and I hiked for three hours and came back happy and tired.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Slažete li se da je danas teže odgajati djecu nego prije?
lines.en: Do you agree that it's harder to raise children today than before?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Djelomično se slažem, i to zato što djeca danas odrastaju s ekranima. S jedne strane imaju više prilika za učenje, a s druge strane teže se koncentriraju. Da to pojasnim: moje dijete brzo nauči nove riječi na tabletu, ali teško sjedi mirno uz knjigu.
lines.en: I partly agree, and that's because children today grow up with screens. On the one hand they have more learning opportunities, and on the other they concentrate less easily. To clarify: my child quickly learns new words on a tablet, but has a hard time sitting still with a book.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Odlično ste to razvili. Za kraj, što ćete raditi kad položite ispit?
lines.en: You developed that excellently. Lastly, what will you do when you pass the exam?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Kad položim ispit, prvo ću proslaviti s obitelji. Ono što mi je najvažnije jest da ću se moći slobodno sporazumijevati na hrvatskom. Baš zato nastavljam učiti i poslije ispita — jezik se ne staje voljeti.
lines.en: When I pass the exam, first I'll celebrate with my family. What matters most to me is that I'll be able to communicate freely in Croatian. That's exactly why I'll keep learning even after the exam — you don't stop loving a language.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bravo! Odgovori su bili puno duži i mirniji nego prošli put. Spreman si.
lines.en: Bravo! Your answers were much longer and calmer than last time. You're ready.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hvala. Osjećam razliku — sada mi rečenice dolaze same od sebe.
lines.en: Thank you. I feel the difference — now the sentences come to me on their own.

days.title: Listening endurance day
days.objective: A full bulletin + a longer audio segment + exam drills in one day.
days.paretoFocus: Exam audio will feel easy if HRT feels normal. Overtrain the ear this week.
drills: Full HRT bulletin in the morning: one fact per story.
One longer HRT audio segment (10+ min) on a familiar theme in the afternoon.
Re-do the DA/NE listening drill in the mock exam in the Quiz tab — aim for 6/6.
Shadow-speak 5 sentences right after the anchor says them.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Retell the audio segment in 4 sentences.
Review any number you misheard today.

activities.title: Izdržljivost u slušanju: pratiti duži prilog
activities.intro: Exam audio will feel easy only if a full HRT bulletin feels normal. Endurance listening means holding attention across a whole segment and catching one fact per story: the number, the place, the time, the decision. These twelve signpost phrases mark the turns of a longer report so you never lose the thread — and so you know where the key fact is about to land.

items.en: in the introduction / at the start
items.note: The anchor previews the stories here. Listen for the topics, not yet the details.

items.en: as we learn / according to what we hear
items.note: A reporting hedge that usually precedes a fresh fact: Kako doznajemo, radovi počinju sutra.

items.en: on the other hand / by contrast
items.note: Signals a turn to a contrasting fact — often a TOČNO/NETOČNO trap hides right here.

items.en: let us recall
items.note: The anchor repeats background you may have missed. A second chance at a key detail.

items.en: it is expected
items.note: Introduces a forecast or plan: Očekuje se više od tisuću posjetitelja. Watch for the number after it.

items.en: according to the latest data
items.note: prema + dative; the number or statistic follows immediately — mark it down.

items.en: in the next report
items.note: A hand-off to a new story. Reset your ear for a new topic, place and fact.

items.en: our correspondent reports
items.note: javljati = to report in; dopisnik = correspondent. Cues a field report with concrete on-the-ground detail.

items.en: in brief
items.note: A round-up of short items — several facts back to back. Attention must stay high.

items.en: as for… / regarding…
items.note: što se tiče + genitive: introduces the topic of the next fact. Što se tiče vremena, sutra slijedi zahladnjenje.

items.en: To finish, here is the weather forecast.
items.note: The standard close of a bulletin. If you hear it, the last facts are the forecast — temperatures and conditions.

items.en: Stay with us.
items.note: A cue before a break; the bulletin continues afterward. Endurance means not switching off here.

activities.title: One fact per story
activities.intro: Exam-style listening under load: several passes over longer items, each time pulling out the single fact that answers the question — the number, the place, the decision. The text stays hidden; you may replay twice, exactly like the real test.

questions.prompt: What does 'očekuje se' signal you should listen for next?
questions.explanation: očekuje se = it is expected — a forecast or figure follows: Očekuje se više od tisuću posjetitelja.

questions.prompt: Listen. How many people are expected at the festival?
questions.explanation: The report says očekuje se više od pet tisuća posjetitelja = more than five thousand visitors are expected.
questions.audioText: Ovogodišnji filmski festival u Puli počinje u petak. Prema posljednjim podacima organizatora, očekuje se više od pet tisuća posjetitelja iz cijele regije.

questions.prompt: Listen. Why will the motorway be closed tonight?
questions.explanation: Kako doznajemo, dionica autoceste bit će zatvorena zbog radova = the section will be closed because of roadworks.
questions.audioText: Kako doznajemo, dionica autoceste između Zagreba i Karlovca bit će noćas zatvorena zbog radova, od ponoći do pet sati ujutro.

questions.prompt: From the same report: until what time is the road closed? (upišite broj)
questions.explanation: od ponoći do pet sati ujutro = from midnight until five in the morning. The endurance point: hold both the reason and the second number.

questions.prompt: What does the phrase 'što se tiče' introduce?
options: the topic of the next fact
questions.answer: the topic of the next fact
questions.explanation: što se tiče + genitive = as for / regarding — it flags the subject of the fact that follows.

questions.prompt: Listen. What is the main change in the weather for tomorrow?
questions.explanation: Što se tiče vremena, sutra slijedi zahladnjenje uz kišu = a cooling with rain follows tomorrow.
questions.audioText: Za kraj donosimo vremensku prognozu. Što se tiče vremena, sutra na cijelom području slijedi osjetno zahladnjenje uz kišu i jači sjeverni vjetar.

questions.prompt: Complete the reporting phrase: Kako ______, radovi počinju u ponedjeljak. ('as we learn')
questions.explanation: Kako doznajemo = as we learn / hear — a hedge that usually precedes a fresh fact.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'To finish, here is the weather forecast.'
questions.explanation: za kraj = to finish; verb donosimo + object vremensku prognozu (accusative). The standard close of a bulletin.

days.title: Reading speed day
days.objective: Two full exam reading tasks under a strict clock.
days.paretoFocus: Slow reading steals time from writing. Target: finish each reading task with 25% of its time to spare for checking.
drills: Timed re-run: both reading tasks from the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
Then: skim one HRT article in 2 minutes and answer who/what/when.
Practise the scan: names, dates and numbers jump out first.
Log timing per task.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Did you beat your day-228 times?
Review 3 vocabulary items that slowed you down.

activities.title: Brzina čitanja: skeniraj prije nego čitaš
activities.intro: Slow reading steals time from writing. The exam reward goes to whoever scans first and reads second: names, dates and numbers jump out before you read a single full sentence, so you know where each answer lives. These twelve words and reading moves build the habit — scan for the landmark, then read only the sentence around it, and finish each task with a quarter of its time to spare.

items.en: skimming, reading at a glance
items.note: Reading fast for the gist. First pass over any text: what is it about, and how is it laid out?

items.en: to look for keywords
items.note: The question tells you the keyword; find it in the text, then read only around it. Names and numbers are the easiest anchors.

items.en: a piece of information, a datum
items.note: podaci = data. Exam questions hunt for one podatak — a date, a price, a place. Scan for its shape, not its meaning.

items.en: title, headline
items.note: Read the naslov first — it frames the whole text and often predicts the questions.

items.en: paragraph
items.note: Each odlomak usually holds one main idea. Locate the paragraph, then read it closely.

items.en: according to the text
items.note: The exam's favourite question stem: answer only from what is written, never from your own knowledge.

items.en: true / false
items.note: The TOČNO/NETOČNO items — find the exact sentence that confirms or contradicts, and beware one changed word.

items.en: to mention
items.note: Se koji se ne spominje u tekstu…: 'which is NOT mentioned' — a common trap wording. Scan to confirm presence or absence.

items.en: summary
items.note: After scanning, form a one-line sažetak in your head. If you can summarise, you understood enough to answer.

items.en: to check the answer
items.note: The spare time is for this: reread the sentence, confirm the podatak matches. Target 25% of the clock left for checking.

items.hr: Imena, datumi i brojevi prvi upadaju u oči.
items.en: Names, dates and numbers jump out first.
items.note: The scanning motto. Capital letters and digits are visual landmarks — use them to jump straight to the answer.

items.hr: Prvo skeniraj, pa tek onda pažljivo čitaj.
items.en: First scan, only then read carefully.
items.note: The two-pass method that saves the exam clock. Never read word by word from the top.

activities.title: Scan, locate, answer — against the clock
activities.intro: Practise the two-pass method on a short exam-style text: scan for the landmark, read only the sentence around it, answer from the text. The last items lock in the reading vocabulary that frames every task.

questions.prompt: What should you do on your FIRST pass over an exam text?
options: skim for the gist and layout
questions.answer: skim for the gist and layout
questions.explanation: letimično čitanje first: get the topic and structure, then scan for keywords. Never read word by word from the top.

questions.prompt: Which are the easiest landmarks to scan for?
questions.explanation: Imena, datumi i brojevi prvi upadaju u oči — capitals and digits are visual anchors that lead you straight to the answer.

questions.prompt: Read: 'Knjižnica Marka Marulića u Splitu radi radnim danom od 8 do 20 sati, a subotom od 9 do 14 sati. Nedjeljom je zatvorena. Učlanjenje stoji pet eura godišnje.' — Until what time is the library open on weekdays?
questions.explanation: Scan for the weekday hours: 'radnim danom od 8 do 20 sati'. The Saturday hours (do 14) are the distractor.

questions.prompt: From the same text: how much does membership cost per year? (upišite broj u eurima)
questions.explanation: 'Učlanjenje stoji pet eura godišnje.' Scan for the number, then confirm the sentence around it.

questions.prompt: From the same text — TOČNO ili NETOČNO: Knjižnica radi nedjeljom.
questions.explanation: 'Nedjeljom je zatvorena' — closed on Sundays. Find the exact sentence; beware answering from habit.

questions.prompt: The question says 'prema tekstu'. What does that instruct you to do?
options: answer only from what the text says
questions.answer: answer only from what the text says
questions.explanation: prema tekstu = according to the text — the answer must come from the passage, never from outside knowledge.

questions.prompt: Put the reading motto in order: 'First scan, only then read carefully.'
questions.answer: Prvo skeniraj, pa tek onda pažljivo čitaj
questions.explanation: Prvo skeniraj (first scan), pa tek onda (only then) pažljivo čitaj (read carefully) — the two-pass method.

questions.prompt: A question reads: 'Što se u tekstu NE spominje?'. What is it testing?
options: which detail is absent from the text
questions.answer: which detail is absent from the text
questions.explanation: ne spominje se = is not mentioned. Scan each option against the text; the answer is the one you cannot find.

days.title: Grammar final sweep
days.objective: A last full pass over the exam's grammar catalogue — strict diacritics.
days.paretoFocus: Tomorrow is mock #2. Tonight your grammar must be boring: genitive plurals, bismo, ćemo-spelling, prefixed verbs — automatic.
drills: Do both grammar drills in the mock exam in the Quiz tab with strict-diacritics mode.
Speed round: 10 aspect choices from the verb families, 30 seconds each.
Re-check your error log: every item should be checked off or re-drilled today.
Early night — mock #2 tomorrow.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Write 'ići ćemo', 'radit ćemo', 'bismo išli' correctly.
Name the 5 exam sections and the pass rule one more time.

activities.title: Završni prolaz kroz gramatiku: neka bude dosadna
activities.intro: The night before the mock, your grammar must be boring — automatic, not thought about. This is the exam's grammar catalogue in one place: genitive plurals after numbers, the conditional bih/bismo, the spelling of futur I (ići ćemo vs radit ću), prefixed aspect pairs, clitic order, and the passive. Read each with strict diacritics and drill anything that still makes you pause.

items.en: five years, six people
items.note: Numbers 5+ and all vague quantities take the GENITIVE PLURAL: pet godina, mnogo ljudi, nekoliko kuća. The classic exam trap.

items.en: 2, 3, 4 + genitive singular
items.note: 2-4 take the genitive SINGULAR (dva sata, tri godine, četiri čovjeka); 5+ switch to genitive plural (pet sati, pet godina).

items.hr: bih, bi, bi, bismo, biste, bi
items.en: conditional auxiliary (I would…)
items.note: kondicional I = bih + participle. Watch the plural: mi BISMO, vi BISTE — colloquial 'bi' for all persons is not standard.

items.en: we will go
items.note: Verbs in -ći keep the FULL infinitive before the auxiliary: ići ću, doći ćeš, reći ćemo. Never contract them.

items.en: I will work
items.note: Only -ti verbs drop the -i and write the auxiliary SEPARATELY: radit ću, pisat ćeš, kuhat ćemo. Two words, no -i.

items.en: to read (impf. / pf.)
items.note: Aspect pair: čitati = the process; pročitati = read the whole thing to the end. Choose by whether the action is completed.

items.en: write / do (impf. / pf.)
items.note: The prefix perfectivises: pisati → napisati (write it), raditi → uraditi/napraviti (get it done). Completed = perfective.

items.en: He gave it to me.
items.note: Clitic order: dative (mi) → accusative (ga) → aux 'je' LAST. je always closes the cluster: Dao mi ga je.

items.en: Have you seen him?
items.note: Order after li: li → aux (si) is skipped as 'jesi li' fronts, then pronoun ga: Jesi li ga vidio? li comes right after the first word.

items.en: The house was built in 1900.
items.note: Passive with the participle: biti + trpni pridjev (sagrađena). Agrees in gender/number: kuća je sagrađena, pismo je napisano.

items.en: Croatian is spoken here.
items.note: The se-passive: reflexive se + verb, no named agent. Ovdje se govori hrvatski = Croatian is spoken here.

items.hr: Kad bih imao vremena, putovao bih.
items.en: If I had time, I would travel.
items.note: The conditional sentence: kad bih + participle in the if-clause, bih + participle in the main clause. Male: imao/putovao; female: imala/putovala.

activities.title: Grammar catalogue — strict diacritics
activities.intro: A last full pass over the exam's grammar: genitive after numbers, the conditional forms, futur I spelling, aspect in context, clitic order and the passive. Diacritics count on every item — mark yourself strictly.

questions.prompt: Hrvatska ima oko četiri milijuna ______.
questions.explanation: milijun and numbers above 4 take the genitive plural: stanovnika.

questions.prompt: Čekam te već ______. (three hours)
questions.explanation: 2-4 take the genitive SINGULAR: tri sata. Only 5+ switch to genitive plural (pet sati).

questions.prompt: Write futur I correctly: 'We will go together.' — Zajedno ______ (ići, mi).
questions.explanation: -ći verbs keep the full infinitive before the auxiliary: ići ćemo — two words, never *ićemo.

questions.prompt: Write futur I correctly: 'I will work tomorrow.' — Sutra ______ (raditi, ja).
questions.explanation: -ti verbs drop the -i and write the auxiliary separately: radit ću — two words, no -i, no fused *radiću.

questions.prompt: I mi ______ rado došli na proslavu!
questions.explanation: 1st person plural conditional is bismo. Colloquial 'bi' for all persons is not standard on the exam.

questions.prompt: Choose the aspect: 'I read the whole book yesterday.' — Jučer sam ______ cijelu knjigu.
questions.explanation: A completed whole action → perfective pročitati: pročitao sam. čitao sam would mean 'was reading' (process).

questions.prompt: Correct clitic order: 'He gave it to me.'
questions.explanation: Order: dative mi → accusative ga → aux je LAST. 'je' always closes the cluster: Dao mi ga je.

questions.prompt: Passive: 'The letter was written yesterday.' — Pismo je jučer ______.
questions.explanation: Passive participle agrees with pismo (neuter): pismo je napisano. Masculine would be napisan, feminine napisana.

questions.prompt: Put the conditional sentence in order: 'If I had time, I would travel.'
questions.answer: Kad bih imao vremena, putovao bih
questions.explanation: kad bih + participle (imao) in the if-clause; putovao bih in the main clause. Both halves carry bih.

days.title: FULL TIMED MOCK EXAM #2
days.objective: The dress rehearsal: pass all 5 sections.
days.paretoFocus: This is the go/no-go signal. ≥60% on listening, reading and grammar + two passes = you're ready to book the real thing.
drills: Take the full mock exam in the Quiz tab: all 5 sections, timed, one sitting, real conditions.
Writing by hand; speaking recorded and rubric-graded.
No aids, audio twice only — exactly like exam day.
Note scores next to mock #1 and day 180.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Log your % per section.
Rest. Analysis tomorrow.

activities.title: Strategija za mock #2: kako izvući svaki bod
activities.intro: Today is the dress rehearsal — the full mock in the Quiz tab, all five sections, timed, one sitting, real conditions. This is the go/no-go signal: at least 60% in listening, reading and grammar plus a pass in writing and speaking means you can book the real exam. Before you start, fix the strategy for each section so no easy point slips away.

items.hr: Slušanje: bilježi na prvom slušanju, provjeri na drugom.
items.en: Listening: note on the first play, verify on the second.
items.note: You hear each item twice. First pass: catch the fact. Second pass: confirm the number, place or TOČNO/NETOČNO.

items.hr: Čitanje: prvo skeniraj, pa čitaj oko ključne riječi.
items.en: Reading: scan first, then read around the keyword.
items.note: Names, dates and numbers first. Answer only prema tekstu — from the text, not from what you know.

items.hr: Gramatika: pazi na dijakritike i padež iza prijedloga.
items.en: Grammar: mind diacritics and the case after each preposition.
items.note: č/ć/š/ž/đ count. u/na + locative for place, s + instrumental for company, od/do + genitive.

items.hr: Pisanje: nacrt, prebroji rečenice, pa lektura.
items.en: Writing: draft, count sentences, then proofread.
items.note: 7-8 sentences per task. Proofread pass 1 = verb endings and cases; pass 2 = every diacritic.

items.hr: Govorenje: tvrdnja + razlog + primjer, tri rečenice.
items.en: Speaking: statement + reason + example, three sentences.
items.note: Never a one-word answer. Expand every prompt to at least three connected sentences.

items.en: Don't leave anything blank.
items.note: An unanswered item scores zero for sure; a reasoned guess might score. Always write something.

items.en: Budget your time.
items.note: Give each section its share and move on. Don't sink ten minutes into one hard item.

items.en: the pass threshold
items.note: prag = threshold. The scored sections need ≥60%; writing and speaking need a pass (zadovoljio). Every section must be passed.

items.en: Take a deep breath before starting.
items.note: Composure is a strategy. A calm start protects the easy points at the top of each section.

items.en: Go back to what you skipped.
items.note: Skip a stubborn item, keep the flow, and return with your spare time — reading and grammar both reward this.

items.en: Record your score per section.
items.note: Log your % for listening, reading and grammar next to mock #1 and day 180 — that's your trajectory.

items.hr: Ovo je proba za pravi ispit.
items.en: This is the rehearsal for the real exam.
items.note: Treat it exactly like exam day: no aids, audio twice only, writing by hand, speaking recorded and rubric-graded.

activities.title: Timed mixed mock — all five section types
activities.intro: A ten-item timed-style mock that touches every section: two listening items, one reading, several grammar structures, and a self-check on writing and speaking. Difficulty climbs 1 to 9. Work quickly and cleanly — then take the FULL five-section mock in the Quiz tab under real conditions.

questions.prompt: Which section demands 'statement + reason + example' in every answer?
questions.explanation: Speaking rewards expanded answers — three connected sentences per prompt: tvrdnja + razlog + primjer.

questions.prompt: LISTENING. Listen. What time does the pharmacy's night shift begin?
questions.explanation: Dežurstvo počinje u dvadeset sati = the night shift begins at 8 p.m. (20:00).
questions.audioText: Poštovani, ljekarna na glavnom trgu radi danju do dvadeset sati, a noćno dežurstvo počinje u dvadeset sati i traje do sedam ujutro.

questions.prompt: LISTENING. From the same message: until what time does the night shift last? (upišite broj)
questions.explanation: traje do sedam ujutro = it lasts until seven in the morning. Verify the second number on the replay.

questions.prompt: READING. 'Gradski muzej otvoren je od utorka do nedjelje. Ponedjeljkom ne radi. Ulaz za studente je besplatan.' — When is the museum closed?
questions.explanation: 'Ponedjeljkom ne radi' — closed on Mondays. Scan for the day, then read the sentence around it.

questions.prompt: SELF-CHECK (writing/speaking). Your životopis has 5 sentences and one missing diacritic. What must you do before marking it a pass?
options: add 2-3 sentences and fix the diacritic
questions.answer: add 2-3 sentences and fix the diacritic
questions.explanation: The task asks for 7-8 sentences and correct spelling incl. diacritics. Both rubric points must be met to pass — then take the full mock in the Quiz tab.

questions.prompt: GRAMMAR. Živim u ______ već pet godina. (Zagreb)
questions.explanation: Static location after 'u' → locative: u Zagrebu.

questions.prompt: GRAMMAR. Putujem na more s ______. (prijatelj)
questions.explanation: s + instrumental for company; soft-consonant masculine takes -em: s prijateljem.

questions.prompt: GRAMMAR (aspect). 'I was reading all evening.' — Cijelu večer sam ______.
questions.explanation: A process over time → imperfective: čitao sam. pročitao sam would mean a completed whole book.

questions.prompt: GRAMMAR. Write futur I: 'We will travel to Split.' — U Split ______ (putovati, mi).
questions.explanation: -ti verb drops -i, auxiliary written separately: putovat ćemo — two words.

questions.prompt: GRAMMAR (clitics). Put in order: 'She gave it to me yesterday.'
questions.explanation: After the fronted Jučer: dative mi → accusative ga → aux je LAST → participle dala. je always closes the cluster.

days.title: Mock #2 error-log review + readiness call
days.objective: Compare mock #2 vs mock #1 and confirm your readiness picture.
days.paretoFocus: Three data points (day 180, 225, 235) show your trajectory. Anything still under 60% gets the final four days.
drills: Full error review: classify, compare with mock #1, celebrate every fixed category.
Check the pass rule per section: ≥60% × 3 + writing pass + speaking pass?
Pick the ONE weakest remaining section for day 237.
Add final missed items to the Words tab.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
State your section scores from memory.
Write tomorrow's plan in one sentence.

activities.title: Analiza pogrešaka: pretvori promašaj u bod
activities.intro: Three data points now show your trajectory: day 180, mock #1 and mock #2. The value is in the analysis. Classify every error, compare with mock #1, and celebrate each category you've fixed — then aim the last four days at whatever still sits under 60%. These frames are the language of a clean error review, and the drill that follows targets the B1 points that most often cost points: clitic order, aspect, passive, conditional and case forms.

items.en: a mistake, error
items.note: greška iz nepažnje = a careless slip; greška iz neznanja = a knowledge gap. Treat them differently in review.

items.en: to classify the errors
items.note: razvrstati = to sort into categories: cases, aspect, clitics, spelling. A sorted log is a study plan.

items.en: to recur, keep happening
items.note: Koja se pogreška ponavlja? A recurring error is worth ten one-offs — fix the pattern, not the instance.

items.en: to compare with the previous test
items.note: usporediti + s + instrumental. Line mock #2 up against mock #1: what improved, what stalled?

items.en: progress
items.note: vidljiv napredak = visible progress. Naming what you fixed keeps motivation high for the final push.

items.en: weak point
items.note: The one section or rule that still drags the score down — that gets tomorrow's focused day.

items.en: clitic order
items.note: The fixed sequence: li → aux → dative → accusative → se → je. A classic recurring B1 error worth drilling.

items.en: verb aspect in context
items.note: Completed vs ongoing: pročitao sam (finished) vs čitao sam (was reading). The choice is decided by meaning, not by tense.

items.en: the passive voice
items.note: biti + trpni pridjev (Pismo je napisano) or the se-passive (Ovdje se govori hrvatski). Recognise and form both.

items.en: the conditional
items.note: bih/bi/bismo + participle for wishes, polite requests and if-clauses. Watch the plural forms bismo/biste.

items.en: case forms
items.note: The endings after prepositions and numbers — the single biggest source of lost points. Drill the ones your log flags.

items.hr: Prag je jasan: ≥60% × tri + prolaz u pisanju i govorenju.
items.en: The threshold is clear: ≥60% × three + a pass in writing and speaking.
items.note: Check the pass rule section by section. Every section must clear its bar — one fail sinks the whole exam.

activities.title: Targeting the recurring B1 errors
activities.intro: The five categories that most often show up in an error log: clitic order, aspect in context, the passive, the conditional and case forms. Fix the pattern here so it stops recurring in the real exam.

questions.prompt: A recurring error is more important to fix than a one-off because…
options: it will cost points again and again
questions.answer: it will cost points again and again
questions.explanation: Fix the pattern, not the instance: a pogreška koja se ponavlja costs points on every appearance.

questions.prompt: CASES. Nema više ______ u boci. (voda)
questions.explanation: nema + genitive: voda → vode. Nema vode = there's no water.

questions.prompt: ASPECT. 'Every morning I drink coffee and read the paper.' — Svako jutro pijem kavu i ______ novine.
questions.explanation: A habitual, repeated action → imperfective present: čitam. pročitam would mark a single completed reading.

questions.prompt: CASES. Idem u kino sa ______. (sestra → instrumental)
questions.explanation: s/sa + instrumental for company: sestra → sestrom.

questions.prompt: CONDITIONAL. Choose the standard form: 'We would gladly come.' — Rado ______ došli.
questions.explanation: 1st person plural conditional is bismo, not colloquial 'bi': Rado bismo došli.

questions.prompt: PASSIVE. 'This bridge was built in 1926.' — Ovaj most ______ 1926. godine.
questions.explanation: Passive participle agrees with most (masculine singular): most je sagrađen. Feminine sagrađena, neuter sagrađeno.

questions.prompt: CLITICS. Correct order: 'Have you told him?'
questions.explanation: li follows the first stressed word (Jesi li), then the dative clitic mu, then the participle: Jesi li mu rekao?

questions.prompt: CLITICS. Put in order: 'I bought it for her yesterday.'
questions.explanation: After Jučer: aux sam → dative joj → accusative ga → participle kupio. The aux sam precedes the pronoun clitics (only 'je' goes last).

days.title: Final targeting: your weakest section
days.objective: One focused day on whatever section scored lowest in mock #2.
days.paretoFocus: Generic review is over. Today is surgical: one section, its task types, its traps, until it clears 60% comfortably.
drills: Re-do that section's drills in the mock exam in the Quiz tab twice.
Re-read the error-log entries for that section; drill each rule.
If it's listening: extra HRT bulletin. If reading/grammar: extra timed text. If writing/speaking: one more timed rep.
Finish with one confidence rep of your STRONGEST section.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Did the weak section clear 60% today?
Review 3 items from the error log.

activities.title: Ciljano na najslabiju sekciju
activities.intro: Generic review is over — today is surgical. You picked the one section that scored lowest in mock #2, and you drill its task types and its traps until it clears 60% comfortably. This catalogue names the trap in each section so you can aim precisely, then finish with a confidence rep of your strongest section. Whatever your weak spot, the fix is the same: know the trap, drill the pattern.

items.hr: Slušanje: broj čuješ jednom — zapiši ga odmah.
items.en: Listening: you hear the number once — write it down at once.
items.note: The trap is a mistaken digit or time. Note on pass 1, confirm on pass 2. Extra HRT bulletins if this is your weak section.

items.hr: Čitanje: jedna promijenjena riječ ruši TOČNO/NETOČNO.
items.en: Reading: one changed word sinks a true/false item.
items.note: The trap is a near-match sentence. Compare the option word for word with the text before you commit.

items.en: Grammar: the preposition dictates the case.
items.note: The trap is the wrong ending. Learn the preposition-case pairs cold: od/do/iz + gen., u/na/o + loc., s + ins.

items.hr: Pisanje: prekratko ili bez dijakritika = pad.
items.en: Writing: too short or missing diacritics = a fail.
items.note: The trap is stopping at five sentences or skipping a č/ć. Count sentences, then proofread diacritics separately.

items.en: Speaking: a one-word answer = a lost point.
items.note: The trap is a bare da/ne. Expand every prompt: statement + reason + example.

items.en: Redo that section's drills twice.
items.note: Repetition on the exact task type is what moves the score, not more new material.

items.hr: Vrati se na svoj dnevnik pogrešaka.
items.en: Go back to your error log.
items.note: Every logged entry for the weak section gets re-drilled today until the rule is automatic.

items.en: drill the case forms directly
items.note: If grammar is weak, drill declension tables aloud — nominative to instrumental, singular and plural.

items.en: Get it comfortably above 60%.
items.note: The goal isn't to scrape the threshold but to clear it with margin, so a bad day still passes.

items.en: Finish with a strong section.
items.note: One confidence rep of your best section ends the day on success — good for morale before exam week.

items.en: Don't introduce new material.
items.note: This late, consolidate what you know. New grammar now only adds noise, not points.

items.en: Every section must pass.
items.note: The rule is unforgiving: one section under its bar fails the whole exam. That's why the weak one gets today.

activities.title: Surgical drill on the trickiest points
activities.intro: A focused set on the highest-value traps across sections: preposition-driven cases, aspect in context, the passive, the conditional and clitic order. Push each until the pattern is automatic and the section clears 60% with margin.

questions.prompt: A one-word answer in the speaking section costs you…
questions.explanation: Examiners reward expansion. A bare da/ne is a lost point — always give statement + reason + example.

questions.prompt: CASES. Knjiga je na ______. (stol)
questions.explanation: na + locative for a static location: stol → stolu. Na stolu = on the table.

questions.prompt: CASES. Vraćam se iz ______ u ponedjeljak. (Rijeka → genitive)
questions.explanation: iz + genitive for motion from: Rijeka → Rijeke. Iz Rijeke = from Rijeka.

questions.prompt: ASPECT. 'She wrote three letters and sent them.' — Napisala je tri pisma i ______ ih.
questions.explanation: A completed single action → perfective poslati: poslala ih je. slala bi would mark a repeated/ongoing action.

questions.prompt: CONDITIONAL. 'Would you help me, please?' (formal, vi) — ______ li mi pomoći, molim vas?
questions.explanation: Polite vi-form conditional: Biste li mi pomogli. bih = I, bismo = we, biste = you (formal/plural).

questions.prompt: PASSIVE. 'The store is opened at eight.' — Trgovina se ______ u osam.
questions.explanation: The se-passive for a habitual action: Trgovina se otvara u osam = the store opens / is opened at eight.

questions.prompt: CASES. Razgovarali smo o ______. (film → locative)
questions.explanation: o + locative: film → filmu. Razgovarali smo o filmu = we talked about the film.

questions.prompt: CLITICS. Put in order: 'Have you already read it to them?'
questions.answer: Jesi li im ga već pročitao
questions.explanation: li after Jesi, then dative im → accusative ga, the adverb već, and the participle: Jesi li im ga već pročitao?

days.title: Writing final: clean copies
days.objective: One perfect, timed copy of each writing genre.
days.paretoFocus: Writing is pass/fail — one clean, complete, 7-8 sentence text per genre is all that's asked. Make 'clean' your default.
drills: Timed: one picture description + one životopis (mock exam tasks in the Quiz tab), by hand.
Proofread pass 1: verb endings and cases. Pass 2: every č/ć/š/ž/đ.
Final native check by your Croatian spouse.
File both as your reference 'exam-day templates'.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recite your životopis one last time.
Recall the picture-description checklist.

activities.title: Pisanje: čisti primjerak svakog žanra
activities.intro: Writing is pass/fail, and the bar is modest: one clean, complete, 7-8 sentence text per genre. 'Clean' should be your default, not your best day. This checklist covers the two exam genres — a description (your day, your home, a picture) and a životopis (CV) — plus the two-pass proofreading routine that catches the errors examiners look for. Make each phrase yours, then write both by hand under the clock.

items.en: 7-8 complete sentences
items.note: The exam length. Count them at the end. Six is a risk; four is a fail. Aim for eight clean ones.

items.en: intro – body – conclusion
items.note: Even a short text needs shape: open the topic, develop it, close it. A one-line conclusion lifts the mark.

items.en: CV, curriculum vitae
items.note: The set genre: tko ste, odakle ste, obrazovanje, radno iskustvo, jezici, interesi. Perfekt with gender agreement throughout.

items.en: a description (of a day, a home, a picture)
items.note: The other genre. Present tense, plenty of locative (u sobi, na katu) and one reason with jer.

items.en: I was born…
items.note: Perfekt agrees with gender: a man writes Rođen sam 1985. godine u…; a woman Rođena sam. The core CV opener.

items.en: I finished / graduated…
items.note: Male završio, female završila. Završio sam ekonomiju u Zagrebu. Watch the case: u Zagrebu (locative).

items.en: proofreading in two passes
items.note: Pass 1: verb endings and cases. Pass 2: every č/ć/š/ž/đ. Two separate passes catch far more than one.

items.en: Check the case after every preposition.
items.note: The most common lost point. iz/od/do + genitive, u/na/o + locative for place, s + instrumental.

items.en: Check the participle's gender in the past.
items.note: As a male writer, every past participle ends in -o (radio, bio, išao) — never -la. A frequent slip under time pressure.

items.en: connectors: because, but, although, so
items.note: Connected text scores above a list of sentences. Weave in at least one jer and one ali/iako.

items.en: Don't leave a sentence unfinished.
items.note: Better one sentence fewer, fully correct, than a broken eighth. Finish every sentence you start.

items.hr: Ovo su moji predlošci za dan ispita.
items.en: These are my templates for exam day.
items.note: File both clean copies as your reference. On exam day you adapt a known shape — you don't invent from zero.

activities.title: Clean writing — the proofreading pass
activities.intro: Rehearse the moves that make a text pass: the CV openers with correct gender, the cases after prepositions, the connectors that turn sentences into text, and the diacritic check. Every FILL is strict — proofread as you go.

questions.prompt: How many sentences should each exam writing task have?
questions.explanation: The B1 tasks ask for 7-8 complete sentences. Count them before you stop.

questions.prompt: Which two genres does the exam set?
questions.explanation: The two genres: an opis (of your day/home/a picture) and a životopis (CV).

questions.prompt: CV opener (male writer): '______ sam 1985. godine u Splitu.' (born)
questions.explanation: Perfekt agrees with gender: a man writes Rođen sam; a woman Rođena sam.

questions.prompt: Case check: 'Studirao sam ekonomiju u ______.' (Zagreb)
questions.explanation: u + locative for place: u Zagrebu. A classic proofreading catch in the CV.

questions.prompt: Proofread (male writer): which past participle is correct for a man?
questions.explanation: A male writer's participle ends in -o: radio sam. radila is the feminine form — a frequent slip under time pressure.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is connected text rather than a bare list?
options: Volim sport. Volim glazbu. Volim čitati.
Volim sport, ali najviše volim glazbu jer me opušta.
questions.answer: Volim sport, ali najviše volim glazbu jer me opušta.
questions.explanation: Connectors ali and jer turn separate facts into connected text, which scores above a list of sentences.

questions.prompt: Put the CV sentence in order: 'After my studies I worked in a bank for three years.'
questions.answer: Nakon studija sam radio u banci tri godine
questions.explanation: Nakon + genitive (studija), perfekt radio sam, u banci (locative), tri godine (genitive singular after 3).

questions.prompt: Diacritic check: rewrite with correct spelling — 'Zavrsio sam srednju skolu.' Give the two corrected words separated by a space.
questions.explanation: Pass 2 of proofreading catches the diacritics: Zavrsio → Završio, skolu → školu.

days.title: Speaking final + exam-day strategy
days.objective: Last speaking simulation plus a calm exam-day playbook.
days.paretoFocus: You know the content. Today locks in composure: strategies for twice-heard audio, answer order, and what to do when a word won't come.
drills: Final relaxed speaking simulation with your tutor or Croatian spouse — enjoy it.
Rehearse recovery phrases: 'Kako da to kažem…', 'Mislim, htio/htjela sam reći…'
Write your exam-day playbook: listening (notes on pass 1, verify on pass 2), reading (scan first), writing (draft, count sentences, proofread diacritics).
Light HRT listening only — no cramming.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recite your exam-day playbook.
Sleep well — tomorrow you certify readiness.

activities.title: Priručnik za dan ispita: mirnoća i oporavak
activities.intro: You know the content — today locks in composure. A calm exam-day playbook and a set of recovery phrases mean that when a word won't come, you keep talking instead of freezing. These twelve phrases and rules are your safety net: how to buy a second, how to rephrase, how to signal you're correcting yourself. Rehearse them aloud until they feel like reflexes, not emergencies.

items.en: How should I put this…
items.note: The classic time-buyer. Fills the pause naturally while you find the word — sounds fluent, not stuck.

items.en: I mean, I wanted to say…
items.note: The self-correction signal (male: htio; female: htjela). Shows control: you noticed the slip and fixed it.

items.en: What's the word for…
items.note: A natural way to flag a missing word and keep going, rather than falling silent.

items.en: Let me think for a moment.
items.note: A calm, complete sentence that gives you a breath. Far better than a long empty pause.

items.en: In other words…
items.note: When the first phrasing fails, rephrase around it. You don't need the exact word if you can describe the idea.

items.en: How would I say this…
items.note: Another fluent filler (male: rekao; female: rekla). Buys a second while sounding thoughtful.

items.en: Sorry, may I repeat that?
items.note: If you tangle a sentence, restart it cleanly. The examiner would rather hear one clear version.

items.hr: Slušanje: bilježi na prvom, provjeri na drugom.
items.en: Listening: note on the first play, verify on the second.
items.note: Playbook rule: use both plays with a plan — first the fact, then the confirmation.

items.en: Reading: scan before reading.
items.note: Playbook rule: names, dates and numbers first; read closely only around the keyword.

items.en: Writing: draft, count, proofread.
items.note: Playbook rule: draft the text, count to 7-8 sentences, then two proofreading passes.

items.hr: Ako zapneš, opiši riječ koju ne znaš.
items.en: If you get stuck, describe the word you don't know.
items.note: The core speaking survival skill: 'ona osoba koja liječi ljude' when you can't recall liječnik. Meaning gets through.

items.en: Take a deep breath and smile.
items.note: Composure is trainable. A breath and a smile reset the nerves and the examiner's impression alike.

activities.title: Recovery phrases and the exam-day playbook
activities.intro: Drill the safety net: the fillers that buy a second, the self-correction signals, the describe-around-it skill, and the one-line rule for each section. These are the phrases that keep you talking when a word won't come.

questions.prompt: A word won't come mid-answer. What is the best move?
options: use a filler and describe the idea
questions.answer: use a filler and describe the idea
questions.explanation: Keep talking: a filler (Kako da to kažem…) plus a description of the idea keeps meaning flowing. Silence loses points.

questions.prompt: What does 'Kako da to kažem…' do for you?
options: buys a second while sounding fluent
questions.answer: buys a second while sounding fluent
questions.explanation: Kako da to kažem… = how should I put this — a natural time-buyer that fills the pause without sounding stuck.

questions.prompt: Complete the self-correction (male speaker): 'Mislim, ______ sam reći nešto drugo.' (wanted)
questions.explanation: Male: htio sam reći; female: htjela sam reći. 'Mislim, htio sam reći…' signals a controlled self-correction.

questions.prompt: You can't recall 'liječnik'. Which describe-around-it strategy works?
questions.explanation: Describe the concept: 'the person who treats people'. Meaning gets through even without the exact noun.

questions.prompt: PLAYBOOK. What is the rule for the listening section?
options: note on the first play, verify on the second
questions.answer: note on the first play, verify on the second
questions.explanation: Use both plays with a plan: first catch the fact, then confirm it on the replay.

questions.prompt: Which phrase best signals you are rephrasing after a failed attempt?
questions.explanation: Drugim riječima = in other words — the signal to rephrase the idea when the first wording didn't work.

questions.prompt: Put the recovery phrase in order: 'Sorry, may I repeat that?'
questions.explanation: Oprostite + mogu li (may I) + ponoviti (repeat). Restart a tangled sentence cleanly instead of pushing through it.

questions.prompt: PLAYBOOK. The correct order for the writing section is…
questions.explanation: Draft the text, count to 7-8 sentences, then two proofreading passes (endings/cases, then diacritics).

activities.title: Zadnja simulacija govorenja — opušteno
activities.intro: Your final speaking simulation, and this one you simply enjoy. Your wife plays a friendly examiner one last time. Read your lines ('Me') aloud, use a recovery phrase where a word slips, and notice how naturally the long answers come now. Tomorrow you certify readiness.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Zadnja proba prije pravog ispita. Bez pritiska — samo razgovaramo. Jeste li spremni?
lines.en: Last rehearsal before the real exam. No pressure — we're just talking. Are you ready?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Spreman sam i, iskreno, čak se veselim. Puno je mirnije nego prvi put. Da razmislim na trenutak što ću reći.
lines.en: I'm ready and, honestly, I'm even looking forward to it. It's much calmer than the first time. Let me think for a moment about what I'll say.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Recite mi, kako ste se pripremali za ovaj ispit?
lines.en: Tell me, how did you prepare for this exam?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Učio sam svaki dan gotovo godinu dana. Ujutro sam slušao HRT, a navečer sam vježbao gramatiku i pisanje. Iz vlastitog iskustva, redovitost je važnija od duljine — bolje pola sata svaki dan nego pet sati jednom tjedno.
lines.en: I studied almost every day for a year. In the morning I listened to HRT, and in the evening I practised grammar and writing. From my own experience, regularity matters more than length — better half an hour every day than five hours once a week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A što vam je bilo najteže?
lines.en: And what was hardest for you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Padeži, bez sumnje. Kako da to kažem… promjene nastavaka su me dugo mučile. Drugim riječima, dugo mi nije bilo jasno zašto se ista riječ mijenja u svakoj rečenici. Ali sada mi to dolazi puno prirodnije.
lines.en: The cases, without a doubt. How should I put this… the ending changes troubled me for a long time. In other words, for a long time it wasn't clear to me why the same word changes in every sentence. But now it comes to me much more naturally.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Lijepo ste se izvukli iz te pauze. Za kraj, zašto vam je hrvatski važan?
lines.en: You recovered from that pause nicely. Lastly, why does Croatian matter to you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Zato što je to jezik moje obitelji. Želim razgovarati s tvojim roditeljima bez prevođenja i, kad budemo imali djecu, želim im govoriti na hrvatskom kod kuće. Baš zato mi ovaj ispit znači puno više od papira.
lines.en: Because it's the language of my family. I want to talk with your parents without translating and, when we have children, I want to speak to them in Croatian at home. That's exactly why this exam means far more to me than a piece of paper.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: To je bio najbolji odgovor dosad. Nema više rough spots — spreman si za pravi ispit.
lines.en: That was your best answer yet. No more rough spots — you're ready for the real exam.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hvala ti što si me slušala svih ovih mjeseci. Sutra bookiramo ispit.
lines.en: Thank you for listening to me all these months. Tomorrow we book the exam.

days.title: FINAL: readiness check + book the real exam
days.objective: Confirm exam readiness in the app and book the official B1 exam.
days.paretoFocus: 240 days from the alphabet to a B1 booking. The certificate (NN 100/2021) is the legal proof of language for long-term residence and the citizenship pathway.
drills: Open the exam-readiness view in Progress: confirm all 5 sections passed and tick the can-do checklist.
Book the real exam: Croaticum (Zagreb), CEHAS or the Rijeka School of Croatian Studies — contact them about 10 days ahead and register with your ID.
Check practicalities: date, fee, documents, location.
Celebrate in Croatian with your spouse — you earned it: Uspio/Uspjela sam!

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say, in Croatian, what you'll do after passing the exam.
Keep the daily HRT habit until exam day — the ear must stay warm.

activities.title: Provjera spremnosti i prijava na pravi ispit
activities.intro: Two hundred and forty days from the alphabet to this: confirming readiness and booking the official B1 exam. This LEARN is your can-do checklist in Croatian — the milestones you can now tick — plus the concrete booking steps. The certificate (NN 100/2021) is the legal proof of language for long-term residence and the citizenship pathway. Read each line, tick the can-do items honestly, then book.

items.hr: Mogu voditi pravi razgovor o poznatim temama.
items.en: I can hold a real conversation on familiar topics.
items.note: The B1 can-do for spoken interaction: enter unprepared into conversation on family, work, travel and everyday events.

items.hr: Mogu ispričati priču i opisati doživljaje.
items.en: I can tell a story and describe experiences.
items.note: The B1 spoken-production can-do: narrate a plot, describe experiences and give reasons for opinions and plans.

items.hr: Mogu koristiti svih sedam padeža sa sve većom točnošću.
items.en: I can use all seven cases with growing accuracy.
items.note: The core grammar milestone: the full case system, singular and plural, with growing accuracy.

items.hr: Mogu odabrati pravi glagolski vid u prošlosti, sadašnjosti i budućnosti.
items.en: I can choose the right verb aspect in past, present and future.
items.note: Aspect in context — completed vs ongoing — across all three tenses. A defining B1 skill.

items.hr: Mogu napisati povezan tekst: pismo, kratki izvještaj, životopis.
items.en: I can write connected text: a letter, a short report, a CV.
items.note: The B1 writing can-do: connected texts on familiar topics, personal letters describing experiences.

items.hr: Mogu razumjeti glavne točke jasnog standardnog govora.
items.en: I can understand the main points of clear standard speech.
items.note: The B1 listening can-do: main points of clear standard speech and many radio/TV programmes at a slow, clear pace.

items.hr: Prijava: Croaticum (Zagreb), CEHAS ili Riječka kroatistička škola.
items.en: Registration: Croaticum (Zagreb), CEHAS or the Rijeka School of Croatian Studies.
items.note: The authorised institutions. Croaticum is at FFZG (University of Zagreb); licensed adult-education institutions also administer the exam.

items.en: Contact them about 10 days in advance.
items.note: Register ahead of the exam date — roughly ten days — to secure a place and confirm the schedule.

items.en: Register with your ID document.
items.note: osobna isprava = ID document (osobna iskaznica or putovnica). You register and sit the exam with valid ID.

items.hr: Pravilo prolaza: ≥60% u slušanju, čitanju i gramatici + prolaz u pisanju i govorenju.
items.en: Pass rule: ≥60% in listening, reading and grammar + a pass in writing and speaking.
items.note: Every section must be passed. The three scored sections need at least 60%; writing and speaking need zadovoljio (a pass).

items.hr: Provjeri praktičnosti: datum, cijenu, dokumente, lokaciju.
items.en: Check the practicalities: date, fee, documents, location.
items.note: Confirm the exam date, the fee, which documents to bring, and where to go before the day itself.

items.en: I did it!
items.note: The line to say out loud when you pass (male: Uspio sam; female: Uspjela sam). Two hundred and forty days earned it.

activities.title: Victory lap — the readiness check
activities.intro: A lighter final set: a victory lap over the milestones you've earned and the booking facts you need. Everything here you already know — enjoy proving it. Then open the exam-readiness view in Progress and book the real thing.

questions.prompt: What does 'Uspio sam!' mean?
questions.explanation: Uspio sam! (male) / Uspjela sam! (female) = I did it! / I succeeded! The line for exam day.

questions.prompt: How many cases can you now use with growing accuracy?
questions.explanation: The B1 milestone: all SEVEN cases, singular and plural, with growing accuracy.

questions.prompt: Which institution in Zagreb administers the official B1 exam?
questions.explanation: Croaticum (at FFZG, University of Zagreb), along with CEHAS and the Rijeka School of Croatian Studies.

questions.prompt: Complete the booking step: 'Javi se otprilike ______ dana ranije.' (how many)
questions.explanation: Contact the institution about ten days in advance to register and secure your place.

questions.prompt: The pass rule requires at least what percentage in listening, reading and grammar?
questions.explanation: ≥60% in each of the three scored sections, plus a pass (zadovoljio) in writing and speaking. Every section must pass.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Prijavljujem se s ______ ispravom.' (personal → osobna)
questions.explanation: s + instrumental: s osobnom ispravom = with an ID document. You register with valid ID.

questions.prompt: Put the celebration sentence in order: 'When I pass the exam, I'll celebrate with my family.'
questions.answer: Kad položim ispit, proslavit ću s obitelji
questions.explanation: Kad + present položim (a future time clause), then futur I proslavit ću + s obitelji (instrumental). Two hundred and forty days, well earned.

activities.title: Zadnja provjera i slavlje
activities.intro: The final conversation of the whole course. Your wife runs one last can-do check, ticks the milestones with you, and celebrates — and together you picture life speaking Croatian with your future children. Read your lines ('Me') aloud. You've earned every word of this.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pa evo nas na kraju. Napravimo zadnju provjeru. Možeš li voditi pravi razgovor na hrvatskom?
lines.en: Well, here we are at the end. Let's do a final check. Can you hold a real conversation in Croatian?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mogu, i to bez prevođenja u glavi. Upravo ga sada vodim s tobom.
lines.en: I can, and without translating in my head. I'm having one with you right now.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Točno! A možeš li ispričati priču i opisati što si doživio?
lines.en: Exactly! And can you tell a story and describe what you've experienced?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mogu. Mogu ti ispričati cijelu ovu godinu — od prvog dana kad nisam znao ni slova, do danas kad se prijavljujem na ispit.
lines.en: I can. I can tell you this whole year — from the first day when I didn't know a single letter, to today when I'm registering for the exam.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Padeži? Sedam njih, u jednini i množini?
lines.en: The cases? All seven of them, singular and plural?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Više me ne plaše. Nisu savršeni, ali koristim ih sa sve većom sigurnošću.
lines.en: They don't scare me anymore. They're not perfect, but I use them with growing confidence.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Onda je odluka jasna. Nazovimo Croaticum i dogovorimo termin za idući tjedan.
lines.en: Then the decision is clear. Let's call Croaticum and arrange a time for next week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Već imam osobnu iskaznicu pri ruci. Javit ću im se danas — deset dana ranije, kako treba.
lines.en: I already have my ID card at hand. I'll contact them today — ten days in advance, as one should.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Znaš li što me najviše veseli? Jednog dana ćeš ovako razgovarati s našom djecom.
lines.en: Do you know what makes me happiest? One day you'll talk like this with our children.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Baš to mi je bio cilj od početka. Želim im čitati priče na hrvatskom i razumjeti svaku njihovu riječ.
lines.en: That was exactly my goal from the start. I want to read them stories in Croatian and understand every word they say.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bit ćeš divan tata koji im govori na dva jezika. Ponosna sam na tebe.
lines.en: You'll be a wonderful dad who speaks to them in two languages. I'm proud of you.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Uspio sam — mi smo uspjeli. Hvala ti na svakom danu ovog puta. A sad, na ispit!
lines.en: I did it — we did it. Thank you for every day of this journey. And now, to the exam!
